reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing immutable conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: Generation runs off the main thread in models/flan-t5-instruct/worker.js via pipeline('text2text-generation', 'Xenova/flan-t5-small', { device:'wasm', dtype:'q8' }). FLAN-T5 is an encoder-decoder (seq2seq) instruction model: the ENTIRE input is one natural-language instruction string (no chat template, no system prompt). The encoder reads it, the decoder streams the answer token-by-token via TextStreamer. Conformance calls-real-inference (source) and loads-clean tested pass; io-shape-semantic is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Beam search (num_beams>1) reorders tokens at the end, so the worker honestly skips the per-token streamer for beams>1 and reports aggregate timing only. No fresh live run performed in this backfill, so no measured latency is claimed.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = the decoder's answer string for a single instruction (e.g. 'translate English to German: …', 'summarize: …'). The worker returns { input, output, inTokens, inTokenStrings, outTokens, intervals, beams, sampled, ms }; the see-inside surface shows the exact input string, its SentencePiece token strings (▁ word marks), and per-token decode cadence.
rubric.notes: no_repeat_ngram_size:3; greedy/sampling for beams===1, beam search otherwise — all real generation options.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/flan-t5-small, linked), task = text2text-generation, backend = WASM, dtype = q8, size ≈100 MB, license Apache-2.0. Conformance declares-model-id/declares-task/declares-quantisation (q8)/declares-backend tested pass. env.allowLocalModels=false.
rubric.notes: The worker matches the declared id; the seq2seq/no-chat-template nature is disclosed on the page.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (shared-auto-init-loader source pass); WASM/q8 with no WebGPU requirement, so it auto-initialises on any device with no Load click.
rubric.notes: auto-init-on-cached is manual-evidenced; progress_callback drives the loader.

rubric.evidence: Loading/progress/ready/error announced in role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); worker errors surface on the page (errors-on-the-page source pass). Runs on WASM everywhere — no needs-WebGPU dead-end.
rubric.notes: Smallest model in the batch (≈100 MB q8), so it initialises quickly relative to the LLMs.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the instruction textarea, max-tokens + beams range sliders, a sample checkbox, sample instruction chips, and Run. The controls map directly to real generation options (max_new_tokens, num_beams, do_sample/temperature/top_k).
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced; sample chips prefill canonical FLAN instruction formats.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the answer, token strings, and per-token intervals all come from the real seq2seq run. no-fake-output is a manual-evidenced assertion.

Conformance buttons-have-names + images-have-alt (script) and live-status-region (dom, [role=status][aria-live]) tested pass at build. The status line is role=status aria-live=polite; the chat log is aria-live. HOWEVER the responsive pre-scan flagged sub-24px tap targets pending agent verification (support=needs-review, automatedNotes: 12 on desktop / 7 on mobile — mostly the range-slider thumbs and small secondary buttons), below this repo's ~44px tap-target bar.
rubric.notes: Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system (public/styles.css). The sub-24px flag is the real open a11y item on this demo.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses fluid flex/grid: controls flex-wrap, the chat/transcript log is intrinsically sized (min/max-block-size + overflow-y), textareas/canvas use inline-size, and the see-inside stat grid is auto-fit minmax. Conformance responsive-desktop + responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) are in the immutable suite. support=needs-review on both classes: the automated pre-scan reported 12 sub-24px target(s) desktop / 7 mobile awaiting an agent matrix pass — no live matrix was run in this backfill. Runs on WASM/q8 on any device (no WebGPU gate); ≈100 MB — the lightest download in the batch.
rubric.notes: I did NOT run responsive-check in this retrospective pass, so I do not claim a measured 0px-overflow result; the honest status is needs-review with the sub-24px tap-target flag.

rubric.evidence: Encoding + decoding run in the Web Worker (inference-off-main-thread source pass); the main thread only paints streamed text and the token/timing readout. At ≈100 MB q8 it is the lightest model here.
rubric.notes: Per-token timing is captured with performance.now() in the worker; flan-t5 has no CSS animation, so no prefers-reduced-motion guard is needed.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread web worker streaming tokens
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep interactions responsive (INP): move heavy work off the main thread; avoid long blocking tasks.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — encoder+decoder inference runs in a dedicated module Worker; the UI thread only paints streamed text and the timing readout.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread pipeline; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks main thread responsiveness
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up heavy synchronous processing so the browser can handle input and repaint.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — for single-sequence (beams===1) generation output streams token-by-token from the worker via TextStreamer; there is no long blocking main-thread call.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: TextStreamer callback posts per-token; beam-search path reports aggregate timing honestly

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; keyboard operability.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live announcements + accessible button names (buttons-have-names pass). EXCEPTION pending: sub-24px tap targets flagged for an agent matrix pass.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region + buttons-have-names pass; support=needs-review sub-24px note

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the instruction textarea and controls flex-wrap with intrinsic sizing; no fixed widths forcing horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-desktop/mobile assertions in suite; flex-wrap controls

openQuestions: Verify and enlarge the 12 desktop / 7 mobile sub-24px tap targets (range-slider thumbs / sample chips) to ~44px, then flip support to ok with a real matrix pass.
Beam search skips per-token streaming (honest) — worth a small UI note so users understand why the beams>1 path shows aggregate timing rather than live tokens.

followUpGoals.goal: Run scripts/responsive-check.mjs at 360×740 and 1280×800, enlarge the flagged sub-24px tap targets, record support=ok per class.
Add a short inline note on the beams>1 path explaining why it reports aggregate (not per-token) timing.

summary: Revision-1 retrospective critique backfill for flan-t5-instruct (Xenova/flan-t5-small, text2text-generation, Transformers.js, WASM/q8, ≈100 MB, Apache-2.0). Evidence grounded in code inspection + the immutable conformance suite (calls-real-inference, shared-auto-init-loader, inference-off-main-thread, declares-*, live-status-region, buttons-have-names tested pass). Distinctive demo: an encoder-decoder (seq2seq) INSTRUCTION model — the whole input is one natural-language instruction string (no chat template/system prompt); the decoder streams the answer token-by-token, with the exact input token strings + decode cadence in the see-inside surface. Beam-search path honestly reports aggregate timing (tokens reorder at the end). Runs on WASM everywhere; lightest download in the batch. GENUINE OPEN ITEM: support=needs-review with 12/7 sub-24px tap targets pending a matrix pass. No fresh live-inference numbers claimed.
